name: LM Format Enforcer (Structured LLM Output)

performance.notes: Minimal overhead; token filtering at each generation step

agent_readiness.notes: Enforces JSON schema and regex on local LLM generation via logit filtering. from lmformatenforcer import JsonSchemaParser, RegexParser. from lmformatenforcer.integrations.transformers import build_transformers_prefix_allowed_tokens_fn. parser = JsonSchemaParser(pydantic_schema.schema()). prefix_fn = build_transformers_prefix_allowed_tokens_fn(tokenizer, parser). model.generate(..., prefix_allowed_tokens_fn=prefix_fn). Integrations: transformers, vLLM, llama.cpp, LangChain. 100% guarantee output matches schema.

gotchas: Local models only (not API-based — use instructor for API models)
Complex schemas may significantly constrain generation quality
Requires access to logits (not available for API providers)
best_when: When 100% format compliance is required (vs prompt-based approaches)
Production local LLM deployment with strict schema validation
When outlines' sampling approach is insufficient for compliance
avoid_when: API-based LLMs (OpenAI, Anthropic) — use instructor instead
Simple JSON extraction where prompt engineering suffices
Very complex schemas (may degrade output quality)
summary: LM Format Enforcer guarantees local LLM output matches JSON schema or regex via logit filtering. build_transformers_prefix_allowed_tokens_fn(tokenizer, JsonSchemaParser(schema)). 100% schema compliance — no re-parsing needed. Works with transformers, vLLM, llama.cpp, LangChain. Local models only (not API-based). More strict than outlines but requires logit access. Use instructor for API-based LLMs.
